# Figure description: Exploration Index Map.png

**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type
Exploration Index Map — a spatial overview map showing the distribution of different exploration program areas overlaid on a satellite/aerial photograph base image. It serves as an index to communicate the geographic extent and type of various exploration activities conducted across a tenement package.

---

## Coverage and Subject
The map covers an area in the **Murchison region of Western Australia**, spanning approximately:
- **Eastings:** 570,000E to 602,000E (MGA/AMG)
- **Northings:** 6,960,000N to 6,988,000N

The area encompasses multiple mineral tenements including:
- **Mining Leases (M):** M 2000128, M 2000129, M 2000305, M 2100086, M 2100125
- **Prospecting Licences (P):** P 2002334, P 2002249, P 2002248, P 2002252, P 2002353, P 2002354, P 2003153, P 2002355, P 2002356, P 2002357, P 2002358, P 2002359, P 2002360, P 2002007, P 2002333
- **Exploration Licence (E):** E 200087

---

## Key Content
The map uses three distinct polygon overlays on a **red-brown arid landscape satellite base**, consistent with typical Murchison lateritic/greenstone terrain:

1. **Pink/Magenta outline (large rectangle):** Covers the majority of the mapped area, representing the zone of **Detailed Aeromagnetic Data Acquisition and Interpretation**. This is the broadest coverage activity, encompassing essentially the entire tenement package shown.

2. **Cyan/Light Blue polygon:** A smaller, irregular polygon concentrated in the **central-eastern portion** of the map, roughly overlying tenements P 2002248, P 2003153, P 2002007, and adjacent areas. This represents **Detailed Geochemical Auger Sampling** — a targeted surface geochemical survey likely designed to detect metal dispersion haloes beneath transported cover.

3. **Green polygon:** An irregular polygon in the **central-western to central portion**, overlying the mining lease cluster (M 2000128, M 2000129) and surrounding prospecting licences. This represents **Mulloch Dump Sampling & Reconnaissance** — indicating historical mine dump sampling and field reconnaissance, suggesting proximity to previously worked mineralisation.

A separate small **green triangle/polygon** is visible in the **southern portion** of the map, around M 2100086 and M 2100125, also classified under Mulloch Dump Sampling & Reconnaissance.

The underlying satellite imagery shows a **laterite-dominated, semi-arid landscape** with occasional drainage lines and pale-coloured salt lake/playa features visible in the southwest corner. The central area shows subtle vegetation and topographic variation consistent with greenstone belt outcrop.

## Research Value
This figure provides a **spatial framework for understanding the multi-phase exploration strategy** applied to this Murchison tenement package, showing that aeromagnetic surveying was conducted at the broadest scale to map structural and lithological targets, while geochemical auger sampling and dump reconnaissance were focused on higher-priority sub-areas, likely centred on known historical mineralisation associated with the mining leases. The presence of **Mulloch Dump Sampling** confirms that historical mining activity (likely gold, given the Murchison context) occurred within the M 2000128/M 2000129 lease area, making this a brownfields exploration project. A research assistant would use this map to spatially correlate different datasets (magnetics, geochemistry, historical workings) and identify which tenements received the most intensive follow-up work, guiding prioritisation of further data review.
